content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem Mex-P Tree. The only difference between this version and the hard version is the constraint on NNN: in this version, NNN and the sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 200020002000.**

The MEX-P\text{MEX-P}MEX-P of a non-empty set SSS of positive integers is defined to be the smallest **prime** number that doesn't divide at least one element of SSS.

For example, MEX-P({4,2,8,16})=3\text{MEX-P}(\{4, 2, 8, 16\}) = 3MEX-P({4,2,8,16})=3 and MEX-P({30,210})=7\text{MEX-P}(\{30, 210\}) = 7MEX-P({30,210})=7.

You are given a tree with NNN vertices. Vertex uuu has the integer AuA_uAu‚Äč written on it.

For two vertices uuu and vvv, we define f(u,v)f(u, v)f(u,v) to be the MEX-P\text{MEX-P}MEX-P of the values written on the (unique) simple path between uuu and vvv in the tree.

Formally, suppose the unique simple path from uuu to vvv is x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xkx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_kx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xk‚Äč, where x1=ux_1 = ux1‚Äč=u and xk=vx_k = vxk‚Äč=v.

Then, f(u,v)=MEX-P({Ax1,Ax2,‚Ä¶,Axk})f(u, v) = \text{MEX-P}(\{A_{x_1}, A_{x_2}, \ldots, A_{x_k}\})f(u,v)=MEX-P({Ax1‚Äč‚Äč,Ax2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Axk‚Äč‚Äč}).

For each uuu between 111 and NNN, define Bu=‚ąĎv=1Nf(u,v)B_u = \sum_{v=1}^N f(u, v)Bu‚Äč=‚ąĎv=1N‚Äčf(u,v).

Your task is to compute all NNN values B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the size of the tree.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the values written on the vertices.
The next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines describe the edges of the tree. The iii-th of these N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers: the values B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
constraints: 1‚Č§ui,vi‚Č§N1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N1‚Č§ui‚Äč,vi‚Äč‚Č§N, and ui‚Č†viu_i \neq v_iui‚ÄčÓÄ†=vi‚Äč
The input edges describe a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.
